workflow.name: Create AI proposals from Fireflies transcripts with GPT-4o, Google Docs, Gmail and Telegram approval
workflow.description:
## Fireflies AI - Meeting Proposal Automation
Listens for completed Fireflies transcripts, qualifies whether a proposal is needed using OpenAI, drafts structured proposal content, populates a Google Doc template, converts to PDF, and sends it to the client via Gmail after you approve it in Telegram.

### Who is this for?
Freelancers, agency owners, and consultants who run discovery or sales calls and want proposals out the door faster. If you are spending 30-60 minutes after every call writing proposals by hand, this workflow turns that into a 30-second review-and-approve flow from your phone.

#### What problem does this solve?
Proposals lose momentum. The longer the gap between a call and a follow-up, the colder the lead gets. Most freelancers finish a great call, get pulled into other work, and send the proposal 2-3 days later when the client has already moved on. This workflow captures everything from the Fireflies transcript, drafts a proposal using your own Google Doc template, and holds it for your approval before sending - so you stay fast without losing control or quality.

##### How it works
1. Fireflies Webhook receives a transcript-ready event after your meeting ends
2. Respond 200 OK sends an immediate response so Fireflies does not retry
3. Fetch Fireflies Transcript calls the Fireflies GraphQL API to pull the full transcript, participants, summary, and action items using the transcript ID from the webhook
4. IF Transcript Valid checks the response is not empty or malformed before proceeding
5. OpenAI Qualify Meeting analyzes the full transcript - determines if this was a sales/discovery call that needs a proposal, and extracts client name, email, company, pain points, services discussed, and scope
6. IF Needs Proposal routes to drafting if qualified, or notifies you via Telegram that no proposal is needed with the reason
7. OpenAI Draft Proposal generates structured content for each template variable - CLIENT_NAME, MEETING_RECAP, UNDERSTANDING_OF_NEEDS, PROPOSED_SOLUTION, DELIVERABLES, TIMELINE, INVESTMENT, NEXT_STEPS
8. Prepare Proposal Data consolidates AI outputs from both OpenAI nodes into a single clean data object
9. Copy Template Doc duplicates your Google Doc template in Drive, Replace Template Variables does a find-and-replace for all 10 placeholders
10. Export Doc as PDF converts the populated doc, Save PDF to Google Drive stores it in your proposals folder
11. Telegram Send Approval Request sends you a summary with client details, a link to review the doc, and Approve/Reject buttons
12. Wait for Approval pauses the workflow execution until you click a button - no polling, no timeout pressure
13. If approved and client email exists, Send Proposal via Gmail emails the PDF with a professional follow-up message
14. If client email was not found in the transcript, Telegram Ask for Email prompts you to reply with the address before sending

###### Setup
- [ ] Fireflies - Create an HTTP Header Auth credential with your Fireflies API key. Go to Fireflies > Settings > Developer Settings > Webhooks, add your n8n webhook production URL, and select the Transcription completed event
- [ ] OpenAI - Connect your API key credential. Default model is GPT-4o for both qualification and drafting. GPT-4o-mini works for qualification if you want to save cost on that step
- [ ] Google Doc Template - Create a Google Doc with your proposal layout. Insert these exact placeholders where you want AI content: {{CLIENT_NAME}}, {{CLIENT_COMPANY}}, {{MEETING_DATE}}, {{MEETING_RECAP}}, {{UNDERSTANDING_OF_NEEDS}}, {{PROPOSED_SOLUTION}}, {{DELIVERABLES}}, {{TIMELINE}}, {{INVESTMENT}}, {{NEXT_STEPS}}. Copy the Doc ID from the URL and paste it into the Copy Template Doc node fileId field
- [ ] Google Drive - Set the destination folder ID in both the Copy Template Doc and Save PDF to Google Drive nodes. This is where proposal docs and PDFs will be stored
- [ ] Gmail - Connect your Google OAuth2 credential. The email will be sent from whichever Gmail account you authenticate
- [ ] Telegram - Create a bot via @BotFather, copy the token. Get your Chat ID by messaging @userinfobot. Enter both in all 6 Telegram nodes
- [ ] Test first - Set the workflow to inactive. Send a manual POST to the webhook URL with a real transcript ID from a past meeting. Review the full execution before activating for production

###### Customization tips
- Qualification criteria - Edit the OpenAI Qualify Meeting system prompt to match your business. Add or remove trigger conditions like budget discussion, timeline mentioned, or specific services
- Proposal tone - Adjust the OpenAI Draft Proposal system prompt few-shot examples and instructions to match your brand voice and proposal structure
- Notification channel - Swap all Telegram nodes for Slack (using Slack node + webhook callback) if your team lives in Slack
- Email copy - Edit the Gmail node message body to include your name, title, company, and any standard sign-off or links
- Pricing logic - The default {{INVESTMENT}} value is [PRICING TO BE CONFIRMED]. Modify the OpenAI Draft Proposal prompt to generate pricing tiers based on scope if you have standard packages
- Batch processing - If you run back-to-back calls, each transcript triggers independently. No conflicts between parallel executions

workflow.name: Fireflies Meeting Transcript to Auto-Proposal with Telegram Approval and Gmail Delivery

values.content:
You are a meeting analyst. Analyze the meeting transcript and determine if this meeting requires a follow-up proposal to be sent to the client.

A proposal is needed when:
- Services, pricing, or project scope was discussed
- The client expressed interest in hiring or engaging for work
- Next steps involve sending a quote, estimate, or proposal
- There was a discovery/sales call

A proposal is NOT needed when:
- Internal team meetings
- Status updates or check-ins
- Social/casual conversations
- Support/troubleshooting calls

Respond in this exact JSON format:
{
  "needs_proposal": true/false,
  "reason": "Brief explanation",
  "client_name": "Name or null",
  "client_email": "Email or null",
  "client_company": "Company or null",
  "meeting_summary": "2-3 sentence summary of what was discussed",
  "key_points": ["point 1", "point 2"],
  "services_discussed": ["service 1", "service 2"],
  "client_pain_points": ["pain 1", "pain 2"],
  "estimated_scope": "Brief scope description or null",
  "follow_up_notes": "Any specific things the client mentioned wanting"
}

You are a professional proposal writer. Based on the meeting analysis provided, generate structured proposal content to fill a document template.

Return a JSON object with these exact keys. Each value should be well-written, professional but conversational text:

{
  "CLIENT_NAME": "The client's full name",
  "CLIENT_COMPANY": "The client's company name or 'your team' if unknown",
  "MEETING_DATE": "The date of the meeting in a readable format",
  "MEETING_RECAP": "2-3 sentence recap of what was discussed in the meeting",
  "UNDERSTANDING_OF_NEEDS": "A paragraph showing clear understanding of the client's pain points and what they need solved. Be specific to what was discussed.",
  "PROPOSED_SOLUTION": "A clear description of the proposed approach, broken into phases if applicable. Be specific, not generic.",
  "DELIVERABLES": "A bullet-point list of specific deliverables (use newlines between each)",
  "TIMELINE": "Estimated timeline for delivery with key milestones",
  "INVESTMENT": "[PRICING TO BE CONFIRMED]",
  "NEXT_STEPS": "What happens after the client approves - onboarding, kickoff call, etc.",
  "summary_for_review": "3-4 sentence summary for the Telegram approval message"
}

Write in a professional but warm, conversational tone. Be specific based on the meeting content. Do not use generic filler.

parameters.text:
⚠️ Client email was not found in the transcript.

Please reply with the client's email address:

parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Prepare Email Data').item.json.final_client_name }},

It was great speaking with you! I really enjoyed learning more about your goals and the challenges you are working through.

Following up on our conversation, I have put together a proposal that outlines how I can help. It covers the key points we discussed, along with a clear breakdown of what the engagement would look like.

I have attached the proposal as a PDF for your review.

Please take your time going through it. If anything needs adjusting or if you have questions, I am happy to hop on a quick call or chat through it.

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts!

Warm regards
parameters.subject: ={{ $('Prepare Email Data').item.json.final_proposal_title }}

parameters.text:
=✅ *PROPOSAL SENT SUCCESSFULLY*

*To:* {{ $('Prepare Email Data').item.json.final_client_name }} ({{ $('Prepare Email Data').item.json.final_client_email }})
*Subject:* {{ $('Prepare Email Data').item.json.final_proposal_title }}

The proposal has been emailed to the client with the PDF attached.

📄 [View Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/{{ $('Prepare Email Data').item.json.doc_id }})

=❌ *PROPOSAL REJECTED*

*Client:* {{ $('Prepare Proposal Data').item.json.CLIENT_NAME }}
*Reason:* You chose to reject this proposal.

The proposal doc is still available if you want to edit and resend manually:
📄 [View Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/{{ $('Copy Proposal Template').item.json.id }})

=⚠️ *TRANSCRIPT FETCH FAILED*

Fireflies webhook received but the transcript could not be retrieved.

Payload received:
```
{{ JSON.stringify($('When Transcript Ready').item.json.body, null, 2).substring(0, 500) }}
```

Please check the Fireflies API key and transcript ID.

parameters.content:
## Fireflies Transcript to Auto-Proposal

Listens for Fireflies transcript webhooks, qualifies the call with AI, drafts a proposal, fills a Google Doc template, converts to PDF, and sends it via Gmail after you approve in Telegram.

### How it works
1. Fireflies webhook triggers when a meeting transcript is ready
2. Fetches the full transcript via GraphQL API
3. AI classifies the call and extracts client details, pain points, and scope
4. If a proposal is needed, AI generates structured content for your template
5. Google Doc template is copied and populated, then exported as PDF
6. Telegram sends you a summary with Approve/Reject buttons
7. On approval, the PDF is emailed to the client via Gmail

### Setup
- [ ] Add your Fireflies API key (HTTP Header Auth credential) and configure the webhook in Fireflies Settings > Developer > Webhooks
- [ ] Connect your OpenAI API key to both AI nodes
- [ ] Create a Google Doc template with placeholders: {{CLIENT_NAME}}, {{CLIENT_COMPANY}}, {{MEETING_DATE}}, {{MEETING_RECAP}}, {{UNDERSTANDING_OF_NEEDS}}, {{PROPOSED_SOLUTION}}, {{DELIVERABLES}}, {{TIMELINE}}, {{INVESTMENT}}, {{NEXT_STEPS}}
- [ ] Set the Doc ID and Drive folder ID in the Google Drive nodes
- [ ] Connect Gmail OAuth2 credential
- [ ] Set your Telegram bot token and chat ID in all Telegram nodes

### Customization
- Edit the AI Qualify prompt to match your sales call criteria
- Edit the AI Draft prompt to match your brand voice and proposal structure
- Swap Telegram for Slack if preferred

**Transcript Intake**
Receives Fireflies webhook, responds 200 OK, fetches full transcript via GraphQL, and validates the response.

**AI Qualification**
GPT-4o classifies the call type and extracts client details. Non-proposal meetings get a Telegram notification.

**Proposal Drafting & Document Generation**
AI generates structured proposal content, populates a Google Doc template copy, exports as PDF, and saves to Drive.

**Telegram Approval**
Sends a summary with Approve/Reject buttons. Pauses until you respond. Prompts for client email if missing.

**Email Delivery**
Downloads the PDF from Drive, emails it to the client via Gmail, and confirms delivery in Telegram.

**Google Doc Template Required**
This workflow will fail without a template. Create a Google Doc with the following placeholders  {{CLIENT_NAME}}, {{CLIENT_COMPANY}}, {{MEETING_DATE}}, {{MEETING_RECAP}}, {{UNDERSTANDING_OF_NEEDS}}, {{PROPOSED_SOLUTION}}, {{DELIVERABLES}}, {{TIMELINE}}, {{INVESTMENT}}, {{NEXT_STEPS}}, then paste the Doc ID into the Copy Proposal Template node.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
